The only way to continue to grow is by helping others become like you. Yes, true growth and fulfillment comes from your contribution to others. Your message has the ability to change someone's life. The impact that the right message can have on someone at the right time in their life is immeasurable. You can help save marriages, repair families, change someone's health, grow a company or more. But only if you know how to get in the hands of people whose lives you've been called to change. Expert secrets will help you find the voice and give you the confidence to become a leader. And he wrote this book about how to make money, he goes and launches this book, he's on every single talk show, we built a funnel for him, he's selling books like crazy, and he's selling these books, he gives 100% of all the money back to charity and feeding families and all this kind of stuff, and people like, man, Tony really loves the world. But then Tony's this attractive character bringing these leads and like crazy, ships him off to this, this company now, who's doing all of the, the investing and that kind of stuff. And I think that I talked to him like six months after the book launched, and my numbers may be wrong, but I think he told me the first six months they raised $6 billion. And this, that was like a year and a half ago, as of right now, I don't know what it is, but like, it's insane. Like there's no other company that can grow like that because most of them, it's just like, it's a company, it's a static boring company, but you wrap an expert in front of it, it's like, it's like fuel, and it makes things blow up. And you can look at most businesses that have an expert on in front of them, and then there's business in the back, and like that's, I think the best and the fastest way to grow. In fact, when we were talking to VCs, I did a whole podcast on this, like how to confuse the venture capitalists, which was really funny. So we're sitting there, and he's like, so what's your cost to acquire a customer? I'm like, well, right now it costs us about $120 to get somebody to join ClickFunnels. He's like, oh, that's really, really like, in their world, like that's a really good number. Like cool, so you just need more money to, to blow to get more people. And I was like, no, we actually turned those ads off. He's like, why'd you turn the ads off? I was like, well, because what we do instead is we build these funnels, people bought my book or this, or they buy things like that. And then those funnels are actually profitable. So we spend, you know, $15 to sell a book, we make $33 through the funnel. So we get a customer basically giving us whatever that, the difference, $13 or $18, but whatever that is. And then we introduce them to ClickFunnels. So basically they come to us, they give us cash, and then we tell them about our product. So we actually pay $18 for every person we introduce to ClickFunnels. And he's like, that doesn't make any sense. So I explain it three or four or five times. Dave was there with me. Like, he's like, this doesn't make any sense. I'm like, well, that's the world we live in. So when you guys understand this, like he said at the end of lunch, he said, if this is true, what you're talking about is actually possible. You guys could change every business across the world. Well, it is true and we are. So it's kind of cool. Suddenly, I'm just like, Tony Robbins is the man. I'm going to go give that dude some money. I don't even care who he is. I don't even care. Like Tony said, it's good. I'm in, right? Like, you don't have to go and spend like six months trying to explain like, why are algorithms better? Why are, you know, all these things, they don't care. They trust you and so they're in. Okay. So simplifies the sell. Next thing, price resistance literally disappears. Okay. Because you're no longer a commodity. Okay. How many of you guys, I probably shouldn't ask, how many of you guys feel like you're in a commodity business where like, everyone's selling something similar, right? The problem with the commodity business is like, that's like the worst business being because like it pushing, everyone's like pushing the price to the lowest thing possible, right? And when that's happening, it's, it's, like that's the worst business in the world to be in, to be like fighting for the lowest price. Dan Kennedy, I heard him once say, he said that, you can't be the lowest price leader in your group. There's no strategic advantage being the second lowest price leader. So you got to be the highest because there's a strategic advantage being the highest. There's nothing in the middle there. Okay. It's like for us, it's like when you build that thing, you just keep raising the prices and price resistance is gone because they want you. They don't care about the thing you're selling. Like, they want access to you and they will pay for that. And then the last thing is free unlimited customers. Like I said, the VC people understand like we're growing our company. It's not costing us any money to grow it. And that's, that's the magic. So that's kind of the, the two sides of this whole, this whole business. Obviously we're focusing on the expert side. It doesn't matter what kind of business you have. Like if you, if you structure it right, it's the fuel for, for both of them. All right. So this right here is what we're going to be covering today basically. This is, these are the three things that you need to build a cult. Sure. And this was kind of a fun thing. So when I was, I've written the expert secrets book three times before we finished it and finally went to print. And a year and a half has been a nightmare. But I think it turned out good, hopefully. But when I was in, when I was in Kenya with my wife and we're a world teach rate and we were in these little tiny van things sitting on this road and they're like, they're all dirt roads. And so we're bumping like crazy as we're going through this thing. And, and it's hard to hear it. Anyone to talk and my wife passed out and everyone else is like bumping in. It's funny. I was like, I can't, like, I can't communicate with anybody because it's so long. We had like a four hour drive in this thing. So I got out a couple of books I brought with me. One was True Believer by Eric Hoffer, which is like how to build a mass movement. And then there was the three or four other ones. I'm going through these books and I'm reading them. And I'm, and I start looking at patterns. I don't know. Like for me, I think I'm really good at, I look at a bunch of stuff and I see these weird patterns like, whoa, people like, do you see that pattern? Like, that's awesome. And she's the pattern that you can model it and you can use it again. Right. So I'm reading all these books and the books were good, but they're kind of all over the place. And as I'm doing that, I'm like, I'm taking numbers next to different things. And I realized that like there were three in every mass movement they talked about in all these books, there were three things that all, all of those had. And it didn't matter if you were looking at which end the spectrum. If you looked at the worst of the worst, you look at someone like Hitler, he had these three things. And he looked at the best of the best, someone like Christ, he had these three things and everyone in between. And it was so interesting looking at how the patterns were the, were the same and every mass movement in the history, since the history of beginning of time, and we'll continue to go forward to have all these three things. Okay. Number one is a charismatic leader. Number two is a cause of something that's happening in the future. And then number three is a new opportunity. And so we're going to go in deep into all three of these because like this is the foundation of it. Like when you have these three things, you can create a mass movement. When you don't have them, you can sell some products, you're the ones you choose and you'll have some stuff. But if you want to like get people to follow you and have, have lasting change, all these kinds of things, like these are the three pieces. And it's been interesting as I've, as I've become aware of these things now, then it's, again, since you become aware of a model or pattern, then you can go and you can like, you can tweak things based on that. Right. And so since then, which has been now probably seven or eight months ago since we're there, we started looking at all these things like, how can we, let's try this. Let's try this. And like, something's worked really good, something's didn't work. We kept doing things on all three of these different things. And, and those changes have been insane. In fact, in fact, those who listen to podcasts, like December alone, we did, we did, like last year was a good year. We did it eight figures, but we did, in December alone of this year, we did half what we did all of last year, which is like, doesn't make any logical sense, but it's all of these things like layering on top of each other throughout the whole year. And then it's like, just things keep growing and growing and growing. And so we're going to go deep into all of these and we're going to be workshopping things, but it's kind of the model and the pattern that if we understand it, then we can replicate it. And then it'll, it's the foundation for all the, all the other stuff that we're trying to do. Okay. Next slide. All right. So as I was thinking through this whole thing, I read an article, I've read this a couple of times. Anyone here read the article, A Thousand True Fans? Okay. So this is, this is by Kevin Kelly. And the article is awesome. Tim Ferriss talked about it in his most recent book as well. But it was just an article or a, like a blog post, something he wrote called A Thousand True Fans. And this was kind of the, the key piece in there. He said that a creator such as an artist, musician, photographer, craftsperson, performer, animator, designer, video maker, author, in other words, anyone producing works of art, which is what we're all doing. Okay. It's a work of art needs to acquire only a thousand true fans to make a living. A true fan is defined as someone who will purchase anything and everything you produce. They will drive 200 miles to see you sing. They will buy your super hot deluxe, reissued high res box set of your stuff, even though they have the low res version. They have a Google alert set up for your name. They bookmark the eBay pages where you opt out of print edition show up. They come to your openings. They have your signed copies. They buy your t-shirts, the mug, the hat. They can't wait till next, till you issue your next work. They are your true fans. And so that's what we're talking about. Like, how do we build thousand true fans? And if you can do that, like, that's when this whole game becomes like, insanely easy. Like, they'll buy from you over and over and over again. And that's when all these fun things happen. And as you start growing that. So for me, like, this has been our focus is like, thousand true fans, thousand true fans. And one thing we're doing right now is we're releasing a lot of like little free plus shipping offers just to get our customers and our fans buying consistently from us. Like, there's something about repetition and I want people to buy from us. I mean, hopefully every day, but at least every week or two. And as we're doing that, we're watching like, we put it out there and as we're watching how many we're buying and pretty consistently, just from like the initial push, we get two or 3000 people to buy every single thing we put, just like, hey, this is live, go buy it. And then they go buy it. And I'm like, cool, like, it's kind of a gauge where we can see like, who are true fans? Who's going to buy my stupid marketing in your car MP3 player, even though they can get every single issue for episode for free on iTunes. Like, they're all free, but like, who's going to buy it? Like, who are my true fans? Like, we find out like, boom, there's the people that and they keep buying over and over and over again. And it's like, you can create things that get people that way. Like, that's the goal. Because you have that, like the article said, like you're set for life. Like, that's, that's when you can say that you're free or whatever you want to call it financially free. Okay. All right. Okay. So the first section here we're going to talk about is the charismatic leader and or the attractive character. How many of you guys right now, guys and gals, are the attractive character in your business? Almost. How many guys there's somebody else that's kind of like the face of it? Cool. So it's like, okay, now we know that. Like, what do we, like, what do we, what do we know about these, about these people? Like, what can we reverse engineer to figure out for ourselves? And the one thing is the biggest thing as I've been talking about this book and sharing more and more people, I get tons of resistance from people like, well, I'm not, I'm not expert. I don't know. You know, and like, there's always that, that thing, right. And, and I understand that. Like the feeling of inadequacy. I don't know if I'm good enough. I don't know if I'm smart enough. I don't know if like, like, I don't know about you guys, but I still feel that like I've been so nervous about this. I put so much time and effort because like, I'm scared to death to get up like, you still me jumping like a little kid back. I'm so excited. I'm so nervous because like, I want to produce for you guys. I don't want any of you guys to be like, man, that was a waste of three days. Right. Like I have the same inadequacies. Like I'm nervous. Like, and everyone has that. But like, and this kind of goes back to talking about the beginning of the book. Like for all of us, like there's a time where we go through this phase. And there's always different phases in life, right. But, but there's a, there's a phase where like, you figured out your thing, your craft and you got excited. You started studying and you like, you geeked out and you got better and better and better at it. But there's a point where like, it doesn't matter how much better you get. Like you can't, like there's a point you can't keep progressing. Right. Like 20 Robbins, if you've been to his event, he talks about like, yeah, I read 700 books in like a year. And he's like, now what? Like, I got like, I know everything, but now what? And you can't like you, you physically, you stop your progression. You cannot move from that point until you help somebody else. The first time I ever saw this was that I ever understood it was my senior year. I was, I'd wrestled all through high school. I was a state champ. Senior year took second place in the country. And I was like, about to go into college. And I was like, I thought I was like the coolest guy in the world, right. And my coach said, hey, you want to come coach at the wrestling camp this summer. I was like, that would be so cool. I think they paid like 10 bucks a day. I'm like, I'm going to be rich. I have almost 100 bucks at the end of this camp. I was really excited. So we go to this camp and he's like, hey, teach these kids how to do the things you're good at. So I'm okay. So I'm teaching these kids and I show them like, this is the move. Okay, go do it. And they do it. I'm like, crap, none of them did it. I'm like, okay, no, do it like this. And they did it. And then like, no one could do it. I'm like, dang it. So I'm like, let me, I'm going to do it real quick and watch me. So I did it. And I'm like, what am I doing? I was like, okay, my, my elbows here, my hips are here. I'm like, Oh, that's why it works. And I find this pattern, right? It's like we're doing now, like I find the pattern. I'm like, Oh, so it only works if my knees here, my hips here and the pressure tight here. So I showed the kids, okay, you got it. And I showed them that. And also they get it. And I was like, wow. And then I realized like, that's the pattern. And then I'm like, I was happening before, like I was, I done so many times, I was good at it, but I didn't know the reason. So in college, I was like, that summer was huge because I learned all the reasons why I was doing things. And I saw the pattern. And as I would go back and become better and better, because I was like, man, if things aren't working, it's because I'm missing one of those three or four points. And I became a much better wrestler because of it. Same thing in this business, like there was a time where I was just like, you see my, those who've done an office tour, like you see the library, it's ridiculous. And that's after we threw away an entire dumpster, like probably four or five feet above the dumpster full of courses when we moved here. So that's like what's left over that made it through the the trash. And like, I went through a phase where I was just geeking out on everything. Like I just consumed as much as I could. I was implementing, I was doing some stuff and having fun. But it was like, I couldn't keep progressing until I like, there's so much stuff, I just wanted to share it. And then like, as I started sharing it, that's when I progressed, right? Like there's a point where the only way you can progress is by doing that and taking that leap of faith from like being a student to being an expert. You want the real people, the people we actually want to work with, you have to create something different. Like the bait is what gets the right person in. So for us, the first bait we created was the 108 split test book, which still sells like crazy by the way. So cool. Then it was just like who it had completely transformed and took off and changed everything. Alright, so I know a lot of you guys have most years have businesses and so but I want to go through this process because I talked about in the book and it was it was a fun experience when we were working through this section in the book and trying to try to really get deep into understanding it and explaining it. And I think it was valuable even if you have a business like just looking at like the market you're serving like if there's tweaks or changes that'll that'll exponentially open up things for you. So as I was going through this, I realized there's only really three core markets. Like every one of your businesses fall into one of these things is either wealth, health, or relationships. There's a fourth one let me know but I thought for a long time and if I actually don't want to know because the book starts in the publisher. So don't let me know but for the most part they're all going to fit into one of these three right. And so you think about it's like like at the beginning of the expert secret world when people started becoming like experts in things like they kind of were one of these three things right was health, wealth, relationships and they were like hey I'm gonna teach I lose weight and all of a sudden everyone's like oh and they like made tons of money right because they were the first dudes and everyone's like oh this is awesome and it was really really easy. But then everyone's like well that guy's making a lot of money I can do that too and they start jumping in soon. What happened is that each of these three markets became this they came the Red Ocean Blue Ocean concept right. It was this Red Ocean now there's 10, 20, 30, 50, 100, 1000 people all in the same thing all going for the for the same fish the same the same thing and so the water becomes super bloody right. And so what happened is that markers business people like us had figured out okay well I need like everyone's fighting for the same customer like how can I how can I shift this to get away from the blood and get into a blue ocean. So you didn't read the book Red Ocean Blue Ocean it's a it's a great book but it's about this this whole concept of like the Red Ocean of competition versus the Blue Ocean where you're unique and you're your own right. And so what happened next is I'm sure I don't know the history lesson behind it but I'm pretty sure this is when things started actually splintering out. So now you got wealth you break it out like inside of wealth there's finance investing real estate sales, internet marketing you know there's a million different like we'll call them in the book home sub-submarkets right. Same thing in relationships you got love dating marriage all things then in health you got nutrition straight training weight loss diet there's like all these things started spreading all these little blue oceans right. So everyone's popping these different blue oceans and for a while like there was one dude who was the sales guy right. I look back the guys I learned from Nish that Kennedy and those guys were like Zig Zig where was the sales guy Dan Kennedy was the direct response guy like they were just kind of their own little blue oceans and that's why they made all this money because they were the only ones. But then they got really good and trained Dan Kennedy trained everybody and then everybody goes out and becomes hit a little hymns and they're all fighting the same pond and suddenly this blue ocean it was amazing becomes all red and choppy and bloody and that's like how do we compete here now right. And so for us like that's the world that we've inherited that's where you guys are at right now. And so a lot of you guys a lot of people they jump in like hey I'm a diet guru I'm a finance guru and they jump into this thing and you're already in red waters like so you can go and you're fighting with everybody else but like you can't it's hard to win when you're fighting with potentially some markets thousands and thousands of people right. And so for you you got to figure out okay we got to go one step further we're in this red market if I picked whatever sub market I've got to go and I got to figure out a new one and I've got to create my own niche. Now I emphasize the word create on purpose. Every person I learned from up to this point we always tell them they go pick a niche go pick which niche you want to be in. If you pick a niche that means you're stepping into a red ocean like if it's something you can pick it's already created there's somebody there so you can step into that but you're not maybe you have less competition but you're still in a red ocean. So you have to be like how do I create my own niche within this. We can create and carve out your spot in this ecosystem. I had a friend that's in the he's a brilliant guy Michael Lovic to make us may know him he's got a couple different companies but he was the first one to explain this to me he said like when you go into a market there's like an ecosystem right and ecosystem is a whole bunch of people like there's like the guy doing this the girl doing this and there's all these different people it's like you can go and like compete like oh i'm gonna do what that guy's doing but then like since you're doing that like now there's this weird like now you're competing and then like all his friends won't like you because you're competing and like clouds up this whole ecosystem makes it like again bloody waters right. Well if you can come to the ecosystem like hey here's the health market all right there's a dude doing that someone doing that like here's all the people and it's like where do I fit if you can find your spot that's different then all the other people no longer like these competitors with you suddenly like they become your best partners and and all these other things like there's tons of benefit from that but it's coming down like creating your own nation figuring out like what like what is your your spot inside of this this whole ecosystem. In the books some examples I was giving I kind of gave out if you look at this so let's do this so here's here's the market from there we'll move to a sub market and then here this is where you're would like get him back and then post him for like a hundred bucks you buy this house horrible like crappy houses we found out this thing where he'd go to eBay and he'd like see a house for like a hundred bucks he'd buy it and then he'd like get the listing and then he'd repost it on eBay and he'd rewrite the headline some copy and put a couple pictures on there and he'd repost it and he'd say he'd buy it for about a hundred dollars and he'd flip it for like three or four thousand dollars he made millions of dollars doing that and then he'd create a whole course on that and so it's like that is like a create like eBay house flipping like that now is this new niche he created that nobody else is doing that right and if he goes back to all the like the house flipping guys and like all wrong people into your world but it's like the it's getting showing kind of behind the curtains of what life is really like that's what gets the right people in like man like that's that's cool i'm gonna be able to hang out with my daughter and drink wake shakes in the morning i want to be able to you know do those kind of things and like having those things out there but it comes back to you like living a cool life like doing cool stuff that you're and letting people see that because that's that's a big a big part of it second thing as a leader you have to maintain absolute certainty satema was in the last inner circle group he did a whole presentation on on self-confidence he said self-confidence is for children like certainty is for men okay if you look at any situation whoever comes into a situation the person who's most certain will always win okay and if you're gonna be the expert and be leading people you can't be like oh wish you wash you know what do you think like you town and back then 13 years ago there's no internet in the city at all um and so we got there and there's no internet and so me I had my little brand new business thing I had like three customers and
